I have a Blazor Web App with Interactive Server (per-page).

I'm building a company intranet. I need ASP.NET Core Identity with
role-based access control. Here's what I need:

1. Set up Identity with three roles: Admin, Manager, and Employee.
   Use an in-memory database for now. Seed one test user per role
   on startup so I can test everything:
   - admin@company.com (Admin role)
   - manager@company.com (Manager role)
   - employee@company.com (Employee role)
   All with password "Test123!"

2. An interactive /admin/users page. Only Admins should be able to
   access this page. Show a list of all users with their roles
   (query from UserManager).

3. An interactive /reports page that both Admins and Managers can access
   but not regular Employees. Show some placeholder report content.

4. An interactive /directory page accessible to any authenticated user.
   Show a simple employee list.

5. The navigation menu should show or hide links based on the current
   user's roles — don't show the admin link to non-admins, don't show
   reports to employees.

6. If an unauthenticated user tries to visit any protected page, they
   should be sent to a login page. If an authenticated user visits a
   page they don't have the role for, they should see an "Access Denied"
   message.

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Add Identity and EF InMemory packages

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me plan the implementation:

1. Add NuGet packages: Microsoft.AspNetCore.Identity.EntityFrameworkCore, Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.InMemory
2. Create a DbContext (Data/ApplicationDbContext.cs)
3. Update Program.cs to configure Identity, EF InMemory, seed roles and users
4. Create login page (Components/Pages/Account/Login.razor) - needs to be a regular form post since auth cookies need HTTP
5. Create access denied page
6. Create the protected pages: /admin/users, /reports, /directory


7. Update the navigation menu to conditionally display items based on user roles
8. Configure Routes.razor to enforce authorization checks
9. Set up App.razor with cascading authentication state

For the Blazor Interactive Server setup with per-page interactivity, I need to handle authentication carefully since pages are server-side rendered by default and only opt into interactive mode when needed. Login and logout should use traditional form posts to work with cookie-based authentication, while protected interactive pages need the Authorize attribute. I'll also need to set up CascadingAuthenticationState and AuthorizeRouteView to properly cascade auth context throughout the component tree.

For .NET 8 and later, the pattern involves using AuthenticationStateProvider, AuthorizeRouteView in Routes.razor, and keeping login pages as static SSR with form submissions. Let me start implementing this now.

data.content:
Clean build with 0 warnings and 0 errors. Here's a summary of everything that was set up:

**Identity & Data Layer**
- `Data/ApplicationDbContext.cs` — EF Core Identity context with in-memory database
- `Program.cs` — Identity services, cookie auth (login → `/account/login`, denied → `/account/accessdenied`), and seed logic for 3 roles + 3 test users

**Pages Created**
| Route | Access | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `/account/login` | Public | Cookie-based login form (SSR) |
| `/account/logout` | Public | POST-based sign out |
| `/account/accessdenied` | Public | "Access Denied" message |
| `/admin/users` | Admin only | Lists all users + roles from `UserManager` |
| `/reports` | Admin, Manager | Placeholder report dashboard |
| `/directory` | Any authenticated | Static employee directory |

**Auth Wiring**
- `Routes.razor` — uses `AuthorizeRouteView`; unauthenticated → redirect to login, wrong role → access denied
- `NavMenu.razor` — uses `<AuthorizeView Roles="...">` to conditionally show links
- `MainLayout.razor` — top bar shows username + logout for authenticated users, login link otherwise
